commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a `classifyProviderError` provider hook, a `plugin-sdk/provider-errors` subpath, status/code/errorType propagation, docs, and regression tests for structured provider failover classification.

PR surface: Source +341, Tests +207, Docs +86, Config +4, Generated 0, Other +1. Total +639 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature/API PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection shows current main has only the legacy provider failover hook, while the PR adds the new structured path and focused regression tests.

target.title: feat: add structured provider error descriptors
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a backwards-compatible structured provider-error contract so provider plugins can classify stable upstream errors without putting provider-specific policy in core.

- adds `ProviderErrorDescriptor` / `ProviderErrorClassification` and a preferred `classifyProviderError(ctx)` hook while keeping existing `classifyFailoverReason(ctx)` support
- adds `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-errors` with `defineProviderErrorMap(...)`, structured `code` / `type` extraction, recovery metadata, and regression coverage for wrapper-code and prose false positives
- passes provider `status`, `code`, and `errorType` through failover normalization into provider hooks while preserving existing context-overflow and status/code precedence
- documents the current legacy flow, the structured flow, backwards-compatibility guidance, and example xAI usage-limit mapping with diagrams

## Why

The xAI feedback was essentially: external providers know their own stable error codes (`SPENDING_LIMIT`, quota errors, billing limits), but OpenClaw core should not grow provider-specific `if provider === "xai"` branches. This PR gives plugin authors a provider-owned way to map those errors into OpenClaw's generic failover reasons today, while also returning typed metadata (`userMessage`, `action`, `retryAfterMs`) that UI/channel surfaces can opt into later without changing plugin code again.

This is intentionally additive: existing plugins that only implement `classifyFailoverReason` continue to work, and the runtime calls the new structured hook first only when present.

## Current flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  request["Provider request fails"] --> extract["Core extracts error text"]
  extract --> overflow["matchesContextOverflowError?"]
  extract --> legacy["classifyFailoverReason(ctx)"]
  legacy --> reason["FailoverReason string"]
  reason --> copy["Generic failover and user error copy"]
```

## New flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  request["Provider request fails"] --> extract["Core extracts message, status, code, and errorType"]
  extract --> overflow["Context-overflow classification stays first-class"]
  extract --> structured["classifyProviderError(ctx)"]
  structured --> descriptor["ProviderErrorDescriptor"]
  descriptor --> reason["reason drives failover and generic copy"]
  descriptor --> metadata["userMessage, action, retryAfterMs for UI surfaces"]
  structured --> fallback["No match"]
  fallback --> legacy["classifyFailoverReason(ctx)"]
  legacy --> generic["Generic classifiers"]
```

## Compatibility notes

- `classifyProviderError` is preferred for new plugins, but `classifyFailoverReason` remains valid and is still called as fallback.
- Provider hooks are scoped by provider id when a provider is known, so unrelated provider hooks cannot classify each other's errors.
- Providerless errors keep the previous generic ordering.
- Context-overflow messages remain first-class for compaction/retry behavior; status/code precedence is preserved.
- `codes` map entries match direct `ctx.code` / `ctx.errorType` or explicit structured `code:` / `type:` fields in provider payload text. Plain prose matching must use `messagePatterns`.

## Verification

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-errors.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/api-baseline.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Provider plugins can map stable upstream billing/usage/rate-limit/auth/model errors into structured descriptors without provider-specific core branches.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `codex/provider-error-descriptors` using the repo test wrapper and full build.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-errors.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/api-baseline.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`; `pnpm build`; docs/API/export checks listed above.

Evidence after fix: Focused unit/API tests passed (209 tests), SDK subpath contract test passed (17 tests), full build completed, API baseline check passed, docs checks passed, and autoreview completed clean.

Observed result after fix: Structured provider descriptors are preferred when a provider hook is present, legacy hooks still work, provider hook dispatch remains scoped, context-overflow behavior remains first-class, and wrapper-code/provider-code cases return the matched provider code in descriptor metadata.

What was not tested: Live xAI/Grok account-limit responses and UI rendering of `userMessage`/`action`; this PR creates the SDK/runtime contract and documentation for plugins to provide that metadata, but does not add a new TUI/Control UI renderer for descriptor metadata.
